steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the overall structure of the text. Map the paragraph: sentences 1–2 describe Native fiction of the 1960s–70s (Silko and peers: realistic, serious). Sentence 3 signals a shift ('Recently, however') to younger Native writers working in popular genres. Sentences 4–5 give an example (Nathan Adler, horror). The structure is: earlier fiction $\Rightarrow$ contrast with more recent fiction.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — It discusses fiction by earlier Native writers, then describes how fiction by more recent Native writers differs from the earlier fiction.: matches exactly — the text presents the older generation first, then uses 'however' to introduce how newer writers work differently. ✓
  • Choice B — It praises one Native fiction writer then criticizes a different Native fiction writer.: the text makes no evaluative judgments; it describes both generations neutrally. ✗
  • Choice C — It provides an overview of Native fiction, then compares it to fiction by non-Native writers.: no non-Native writers appear anywhere in the text. ✗
  • Choice D — It describes one Native author's early works, then describes her more recent works.: the text covers two different generations of writers, not two phases of one author's career; Silko and Adler are separate people. ✗
That is the correct answer.
